# CloverSec CTF For Example 1.0.14

This release fixes natural-prompt collection behavior exposed by real 1.0.13 testing.

- Workflow search now records `continuable_cases` and does not treat WP-only, solver-only, platform-page-only, or README-only leads as deliverable challenge material.
- `collect`, `download_preview`, and `download_accept` now stop when `ctf_cases.jsonl` contains only non-deliverable leads, so README/WP files cannot be accepted as challenge handout material.
- The one-shot workflow runner now stops on `pending_user`, `blocked`, `incomplete`, or `failed` stages instead of continuing into later stages and creating misleading process output.
- Final report generation now requires real local material or an archive manifest before creating a human-facing delivery folder.
- Material collection filters GitHub/direct URL previews to results that can plausibly produce source, attachments, official challenge metadata, release assets, or a challenge tree.
- Dockerizer handoff now recommends the inferred source entry directory, such as `challenge_source/`, instead of the outer work folder.

Validation:

- `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py'`
- `python3 /Users/d1a0y1bb/.codex/skills/.system/plugin-creator/scripts/validate_plugin.py plugins/cloversec-ctf-forexample`
- `python3 scripts/validate_release.py`
- Real repackage checks against the JSFS and zoo feedback form output folders.
- Simulated natural-prompt WP-only workflow now stops at `research:pending_user` and creates no accepted downloads.
